MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kelly Breen of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2204), appearing for defendant / moving party Carrier Corporation
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Terrance & Patricia Peterson

RULING:
DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Carrier Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claims and Mr. Peterson's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Even assuming Admiral Horne's declaration is admissible, when read in conjunction with Mr. Peterson's deposition testimony it merely creates a triable issue whether the gaskets Mr. Peterson removed from Carrier compressors were original. Additionally, defendant did not submit evidence to demonstrate that Mr. Peterson did not install replacement gaskets for which defendant is liable nor to demonstrate that Mr. Peterson was not exposed during the installation of replacement gaskets. Plaintiffs' objections to the Horne declaration in its entirety and to paragraphs 9 and 10 are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Peterson's deposition testimony are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
